methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Axibo AI is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised C$12M to date. The comparable universe for this sector includes one public company (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers, with notable private comparables including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median valuation method is not applicable to this analysis. The robotics and manufacturing sector contains 11 same-sector peers overall, but insufficient data exists within narrowly filtered cohorts—zero companies matched both same-stage and similar-valuation-tier criteria simultaneously, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for reliable triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would risk conflating companies across disparate development stages and market positions, introducing material bias.

Given the inapplicability of primary valuation methods, this analysis cannot support a defensible valuation range at this time. Future analysis would require either (1) expansion of the comparable set to include adjacent sectors with analogous unit economics and capital intensity, (2) gathering additional financial metrics from existing private peers to enable revenue or EBITDA multiples analysis, or (3) reliance on alternative approaches such as venture capital method calibration against sector-specific risk and return expectations. The primary uncertainty remains the absence of sufficient homogeneous comparables to benchmark Axibo AI's stage, capital efficiency, and market positioning.
